segments.text: Every trader at SMB Capital goes through these exact five levels.
You know, most people get stuck at level three and never progress.
Let me show you where you are and how to move up.
Because level three, that's where most trading careers go to die.
And you probably don't even realize you're there.
But before we talk through these five levels, I need you to do something.
Be brutally honest with yourself about where you actually are.
Not where you want to be.
Not where you think you should be, where you actually are.
Because here's what I see all the time.
Traders lying to themselves about which level they're on.
We have a saying on the desk, we got it from one of the most tenured traders here.
He's worked with many of the best traders in the world, independent traders, elite prop traders.
Traders that really well know and hedge funds.
Like he's seen what makes traders great.
He's also seen what holds them back.
And he says the difference maker is two words.
Brutal honesty.
You have to be brutally honest with yourself.
I'm asking you to do that right now.
If you can't, please turn off this video, go watch something else.
But if you want to move forward, commit to it.
Pause the video right now and write in the comments.
I'm going to be brutally honest with myself.
With brutal honesty, you can move forward without it.
You'll be a level two trader convincing yourself you're a level four.
And that lie that just keeps traders stuck.
So as we go through these levels, I want you to identify yourself honestly, even if it's uncomfortable.
Because once you know where you are, I can show you exactly how to get to the next level.
And let's start at the beginning.
Here's how this works.
Every level has three characteristics.
Mindset.
Behavior.
And P&L pattern.
And here's the critical part.
You can't skip levels.
You can't go from level one to level four.
You have to progress through these each one.
Because each level teaches you something you need for the next one.
And level three, it's the danger zone.
This is where a lot of traders spend years grinding,
making just enough progress to not quit,
but not enough progress to actually break through.
I'm going to show you how to recognize if you're stuck there.
And what it takes to get out.
All right, let's go through all five.
Level one is the gambler.
The mindset of the gambler is I can feel which way this is going to go.
The behavior is you're taking trades based on gut feeling.
No real written rules, no actual plan.
You see the stock moving and you just kind of jump in.
Or even worse, you see the stock not moving and hope it starts to move and you jump in.
Sometimes you win and when you win, you think you're a genius.
You feel great about yourself.
You start smelling your own perfume, right?
Most of the time you lose though and you don't actually know why.
The P&L pattern of the gambler is chaotic.
It's big swings.
You're up three thousand one day.
You're down four five hundred the next.
There's no consistency.
But how do you graduate from level one?
Three steps.
Write down your entry rules.
Write down your exit rules.
This is for every trade you take.
Write down your entry rule.
Write down your exit rule.
And follow them for 30 days.
That's it.
Time investment is one month before you take any trade.
Write down your entry rule.
Write down your exit rule and follow it for 30 days.
Do not take a trade until you write down your entry rule.
Write down your exit rule and make sure you follow it.
So here's the problem.
Even when we tell people to do this, most people spend six months here because they start.
And then they see some improvement, right?
And then they stop following this rule.
They stop following the subjective.
They start to backslide.
You lose the brutal honesty that forces discipline.
The fitness industry survives on this exact behavior.
People make progress and then they abandon the habit.
And then they slide right back to where they started.
Some traders spend two, three, even five years here.
Just in total randomness, but you don't have to.
The faster you admit your gambling, the faster you're going to move to level two.
Let's talk about level two.
It's the system seeker.
The mindset of a level two trader is I just need to find the right strategy.
If I could just find a better strategy, I'll be in the best place, right?
You're reading every book.
You're taking every course.
You're back testing strategies.
You have all the rules now.
You can understand every indicator and how they move.
Why they're on their chart, everything else.
But you keep changing what your focal point is.
Every time a strategy even shows signals of stop working, you abandon it and you go and find a new one.
You're essentially just a strategy tourist, right?
Your P&L pattern.
Well, a lot of it's more controlled, but you're still losing smaller losses, certainly winning days, but net net.
You're typically still red or even just chopping around a little bit.
Here's what changes at level two.
You're learning the language of trading.
You've learned support.
You've learned resistance.
You've learned VWOP, volume.
You're learning about trading.
But you're not yet learning how to trade.
Here's how you graduate from level two.
You pick one strategy, only one.
You pick a single strategy with scalpel radar.
We teach you five.
You pick a single one and then you trade it for 20 reps, 20 focused reps.
No switching, no looking in other strategies, 20 reps.
You notice this isn't time-based.
Why isn't it time-based?
Because you can't predict how often you're going to get that trade.
But you get a number, 20.
Most quants actually think of it as like 35.
We've found that if you're studying your trading, you can actually get to 20 and gather all the important information you need.
Most traders can do this under a month because this level teaches discipline.
And it teaches you something more important than the quality of the strategy.
The strategy isn't the problem.
Your execution is the problem.
You need to stick with this for 20 focused reps.
You'll learn more about the strategy and even more about yourself if you do this.
Once you're through that 20 focused reps, we go right to level three.
This is the level I warned you about, right?
Level three is the break even grinder.
This is the cruelest level because you're so close, you can feel yourself so close.
The mindset of level three is I'm doing everything right.
Why am I not profitable?
The behavior of level three is you have a strategy.
You follow your rules mostly.
You're managing your risk.
You're reviewing your trades.
You have winning weeks.
You have losing weeks.
But at the end of the month, you're basically break even.
Maybe even up a little, maybe down a little.
But just kind of stuck.
Your P&L pattern is observably flat.
You might have a little run up, but then it comes right back down.
Once something that's fascinating about level three is you know enough not to go negative
or not to continually slide down in your P&L pattern.
So it winds up being flat.
Grinding, no momentum.
One trader and our Jess Jackie spent months at level three.
Jackie would have a great week, make $18,000.
Think this is it.
I figured it out.
Next week, lose 2000.
The next week, lose 5,000.
The next week, lose 12,000.
Back to zero.
And here's what made it so frustrating.
Jackie was following the rules, managing risk, doing the work.
So why wasn't it working?
So that's Jackie one question.
Show me your last 10 trades.
We looked at them for maybe 60 seconds, right?
I'm not talking about each individual execution, each trading idea.
Show me the last 10, right?
After 60 seconds, I just said, you're following your rules perfectly,
but you're not enforcing your standard.
Jackie looked at me a little puzzled and said, what's the difference?
I told you, your rules tell you when to enter an exit.
Your standards tell you which setups are worth taking.

but half of them are just C setups and C setups,
unfortunately, sized at B risk, produce breaking even results.

Not one for you.
Jackie actually wasn't breaking rules.
Jackie just had no quality filter.
Jackie's size trades based on their recent results.
Everything was about Jackie's results.
Jackie would start doing well.
Jackie would start sizing up.
Jackie would start doing poorly.
Jackie would start sizing down.
None of the focus was on the opportunity created by the market.

but not all opportunities are equal.

Does it meet my technical criteria?
Is the risk reward at least three to one?
Now think about this.
Does it meet my technical criteria?
Is the setup and the trade actually there?

This one holds a lot of people up because you're like,
what about one to one scouts?

do I clearly see the opportunity?

do I feel the opportunity the right way?
What's fascinating about this little three-step process?
Does it fit my technical criteria?
Is the risk reward at least three to one?
And do I clearly see and feel the opportunity?
What they were doing with this,
Jackie created a grading system.
Here's how.
None of this was hope-based.
None of it was about how Jackie's performing.
Every trade started with zero risk.

Jackie let the market earn the risk
that was going to go into the trade.
Jackie didn't say,
I'm going to size up or size down based on how I'm trading.
Jackie said, I'm going to size up or size down
because the market is telling me
that there's a better opportunity here.
Just using that simple three-step process.
First month with this new filter,
Jackie took 11 trades instead of 43.
Made something like $42,000, right?
Not because the setup changed,
but the bet sizing changed.
That was the level three breakthrough.
Quality over quantity.
Jackie didn't need more trades.
No one really needs more trades.
You need better trades.
You start with zero risk.
Anything less than a C gets zero risk.
Your default isn't B-risk.
Your default is zero.
And then if you have the setups that check in your favor,
if you have those three criteria starting to be met,
your C setups get 5% of a daily stop.
Your B setups get 15% of a daily stop.
Your A setups get 30% of a daily stop.
And then your A plus setups,
they get 80% of a daily stop.
This is exactly how you graduate from level three.
You add a quality filter.
Not just does this trade meet my rules
and how am I doing in my trading,

What's fascinating is once you use this structure,
I mean, people spend years at this level three

the time invested here tends to be about two months.
